title: Rich Saudi Lamb Biryani
description: A savory, umami-packed lamb biryani that balances spices and aromatics for a dense, romantic dish, all while being sugar‑free friendly.

ingredients.name: Mixed spice powder (cumin 1 tsp, coriander 1 tsp, garam masala 1 tsp, red chili flakes ½ tsp, Kashmiri chili powder 1 tsp)

instructions: Rinse the basmati rice under cold water until the water runs clear, then soak it for 30 minutes and drain.
Combine the lamb cubes with yogurt, ginger garlic paste, salt, pepper, and the mixed spice powder; marinate in the refrigerator for at least 30 minutes to let the flavors meld.
Heat the ghee in a heavy, deep‑sided pot over medium heat. Add the sliced onions and sauté until they turn golden brown and caramelized, about 8 minutes.
Add the marinated lamb to the pot and sear each piece until it develops a deep brown crust, about 5 minutes per side.
Stir in the diced tomato and cook until the tomatoes soften and release their juices, about 3 minutes.
Pour in 600 ml of water, bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer the lamb for 25 minutes until it becomes tender.
Meanwhile, bring 600 ml of water to a boil in a separate pot. Add the soaked rice and cook until it is al dente (about 5–7 minutes). Drain the rice and set aside.
Layer the partially cooked rice over the simmering lamb in the main pot. Sprinkle the chopped mint and cilantro evenly over the rice layer.

Cover the pot with a tight‑sealing lid and a piece of parchment paper or foil to trap moisture. Steam the layered rice and lamb over low heat (around 165 °C) for 15 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld and the rice to finish cooking.

Fluff the rice gently with a fork, mix the layers lightly, and garnish with the remaining mint and cilantro leaves. Serve the biryani hot, accompanied by a side of plain yogurt or a simple green salad if desired.
